content.sentence: Cô ấy rất ___ với mọi người.

translation.en: She is very tolerant with everyone.

notes_md:
**chịu** = to accept, to agree to do something, or to endure/suffer something. It often implies you’re not super happy about it, but you go along with it.

Very common pattern for willingness:  
`Structure: chịu + V` (chịu đi, chịu thử) → be willing to do something.  
With **không**, it often means refuse.

It also means “endure / stand” something physical or emotional.  
`Structure: chịu được + N/V` (chịu được đau) → can stand / tolerate.

Strong memory link: **đồng ý** (agree) is more neutral; **từ chối** (refuse) is the clear opposite of *chịu*.
exampleSentencesTranslated: He does not want to accept responsibility for this.
If you do not accept studying, the results will not be good.
Parents always hope their children accept working hard.

definitions.en: To bear, to endure, or to take responsibility.
definitions.vi: Chịu đựng, gánh vác hoặc đảm nhận trách nhiệm.

translations.vi: chịu đựng, gánh vác, đảm nhận trách nhiệm
translations.en: to bear, to endure, to take responsibility

example.vi: Tôi chịu trách nhiệm cho dự án này.
example.en: I take responsibility for this project.
example.es: Yo asumo la responsabilidad de este proyecto.

exampleSentences: Anh không muốn chịu trách nhiệm về việc này đâu.
Nếu em không chịu học, kết quả sẽ không tốt đâu.
Bố mẹ luôn mong con chịu khó làm việc chăm chỉ.

It also means “endure / stand” something physical or emotional.  
Structure: chịu được + N/V (chịu được đau) → can stand / tolerate.
